reason: The text fragment (Exodus 7-12) primarily depicts Moses and Aaron, the Hebrew leaders, delivering God's messages to Pharaoh and performing signs. Their interactions with the people shown in this passage involve giving instructions about the Passover (selecting the lamb, marking doorposts, eating in haste, etc.), to which the people obey ('וַיִּקֹּ֥ד הָעָ֖ם וַיִּֽשְׁתַּחֲוּֽוּ׃' - 'and the people bowed and worshipped'). There is no instance in this text where Hebrew leaders express rage or anger toward the people; instead, they act as intermediaries of divine authority.
